description: Give quantmeet a real WebRTC client. The backend uses livekit-server-sdk, but the frontend has NO livekit-client / getUserMedia / RTCPeerConnection: VideoTile.tsx renders avatar initials, PreJoinLobby.tsx renders the literal text 'Camera preview', handleJoin just flips local state, chat is local-only, and /api/meetings/recent 404s. This is the largest flagship gap.
steps: Add livekit-client to apps/quantmeet. Add a backend route that mints a LiveKit access token (using livekit-server-sdk + LIVEKIT_API_KEY/SECRET) for a given room+identity; expose via /api/meetings/[id]/token.
PreJoinLobby.tsx: request getUserMedia({video,audio}), render the real local MediaStream in a <video> element, add device pickers + mute/camera toggles; replace the 'Camera preview' placeholder.
On join: connect livekit-client Room to LIVEKIT_WS_URL with the minted token; publish local tracks; subscribe to remote participants.
VideoTile.tsx: attach the participant's video/audio MediaStreamTrack to a <video>/<audio> element (fallback to initials only when camera off).
Wire participant list, active-speaker, mute state from Room events; make chat use the real data channel or the realtime backend instead of local setChatMessages.
Add the missing /api/meetings/recent route (backend) so the home screen stops 404ing.
Add tests for the token endpoint and a component test that VideoTile attaches a provided MediaStream. Run pnpm --filter @quant/quantmeet typecheck test build.
acceptance_criteria: PreJoinLobby shows a real camera preview via getUserMedia
Joining connects a livekit-client Room with a server-minted token; remote video/audio render in VideoTile
Mute/camera/participant state driven by Room events; chat uses real transport
Token endpoint + VideoTile tests pass; typecheck/build green
